symptom: Effective maximum range has degraded ~8% over the past week (about 110 m vs a 120 m baseline). Diagnose the root cause.

hypotheses.label: Window contamination reducing transmitted / received optical power
Detector sensitivity drift (dark-current rise or responsivity loss)
Laser aging / output power decline
Thermal / TEC fault causing elevated operating temperature that degrades SNR
Calibration / signal-processing drift (range-gate offset or gain table error)
Scanner / power (shared upstream cause)
Window contamination reducing transmitted / received optical power
Detector sensitivity drift (dark-current rise or responsivity loss)
Laser aging / output power decline
Thermal / TEC fault causing elevated operating temperature that degrades SNR
Calibration / signal-processing drift (range-gate offset or gain table error)
Scanner / power (shared upstream cause)

evidence.summary: Laser output has dropped 12.2% (42.4 mW vs 48.2 mW baseline). Critically, diode temperature is 30.96 °C — well above the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum — indicating a TEC or thermal-management fault is suppressing laser power, not intrinsic laser aging. A 12.2% optical power reduction is sufficient to account for the observed ~8% range degradation (range scales sub-linearly with transmitted power), making additional optical-path or detector-side causes less necessary to invoke.

hypotheses.label: Window contamination reducing transmitted / received optical power

Laser aging / output power decline
Thermal / TEC fault causing elevated operating temperature that degrades SNR
Calibration / signal-processing drift (range-gate offset or gain table error)
Scanner / power (shared upstream cause)

evidence.summary: Laser output has dropped 12.2% (42.4 mW vs 48.2 mW baseline). Critically, diode temperature is 30.96 °C — well above the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum — indicating a TEC or thermal-management fault is suppressing laser power, not intrinsic laser aging. A 12.2% optical power reduction is sufficient to account for the observed ~8% range degradation (range scales sub-linearly with transmitted power), making additional optical-path or detector-side causes less necessary to invoke.

TEC current is 2.27 A — 91% of the 2.5 A hardware limit — yet the diode is held at 30.96 °C, which is 4.96 °C above the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum, and the controller flag losing_setpoint=true confirms active thermal runaway. The TEC is saturating its drive capacity and still cannot recover the setpoint, pointing to a failing TEC or an increased thermal load the TEC cannot reject, not intrinsic laser aging. This is consistent with ev.laser_power_01: suppressed laser output is a downstream consequence of the elevated junction temperature forced by TEC loss-of-control.

TEC current is 2.27 A — 91% of the 2.5 A hardware limit — yet the diode is held at 30.96 °C, which is 4.96 °C above the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum, and the controller flag losing_setpoint=true confirms active thermal runaway. The TEC is saturating its drive capacity and still cannot recover the setpoint, pointing to a failing TEC or an increased thermal load the TEC cannot reject, not intrinsic laser aging. This is consistent with ev.laser_power_01: suppressed laser output is a downstream consequence of the elevated junction temperature forced by TEC loss-of-control.

common_mode_check returned common_mode=false with 3 degraded channels and an onset spread of 4.25 days. The staggered, non-simultaneous multi-channel onset pattern is incompatible with a single shared upstream event (power rail, scanner drive) that would degrade all channels in lockstep. It modestly disfavours h4 in the multi-channel reading (a single TEC unit fault should not produce staggered onset across 3 channels) though h4 remains dominant via direct thermal evidence. Independent per-channel staggered onsets are marginally more consistent with independent per-channel processes (h2, h3). The lack of common mode further corroborates ruling out h1 (a contaminated window would be common-mode) and h5 (a gain-table or range-gate error would be system-wide).

log.reboot: degradation onset predates it → coincident, not causal
detector_temp_C is stuck (≈zero variance) → unreliable channel

steps.recommended_action: Inspect and replace the TEC assembly: ev.tec_load_01 shows TEC current saturated at 2.27 A (91% of 2.5 A limit) with losing_setpoint=true and diode held at 30.96 °C — 4.96 °C above the 26.0 °C max setpoint — indicating the TEC can no longer reject heat. This thermal runaway is the proximate cause of the 12.2% laser power drop confirmed in ev.laser_power_01, which is sufficient to account for the 8% range degradation; intrinsic laser aging (h3) is not independently implicated. The staggered 4.25-day onset across 3 channels (ev.common_mode_01) suggests per-channel TEC degradation rather than a single shared unit — audit each channel's TEC drive current and thermal interface independently rather than assuming a single common failure. Do not use detector_temp_C readings for thermal baselining: the channel is stuck with near-zero variance and is unreliable per ev.stuck_detector_temp_C. The system reboot (ev.trigger_demoted) postdates degradation onset and is non-causal.

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

No evidence has been collected yet and all six hypotheses are at equal log_odds. Laser output power is the single measurement that best partitions the hypothesis space at this stage. A degraded reading raises h3 (laser aging) and makes h4 (thermal/TEC fault driving laser output down) jointly plausible, while simultaneously arguing against h1, h2, and h5. A nominal reading does the opposite: it rules out h3, partially argues against hyp.upstream_power (TX power branch), and shifts probability mass toward h1 (window — attenuates both TX and RX), h2 (detector sensitivity loss — RX-side only), h4 (thermal degrading SNR via detector dark current rather than laser power), and h5 (calibration/gain drift — no optical loss at all). No prior evidence exists to cite; this check produces the first discriminating data point and avoids re-confirming anything already known.

ev.laser_power_01 establishes that diode temperature is 4.96 °C above its 26.0 °C setpoint maximum, implicating a thermal/TEC failure (h4) as the proximate cause of the 12.2% laser power drop. However, hyp.upstream_power (shared upstream supply issue) is equally scored at log_odds=0.00 and could explain the same temperature elevation via under-voltage to the TEC driver rather than a TEC fault per se. tec_load_check will reveal TEC drive current, duty cycle, and supply rail health: (a) an overloaded or degraded TEC running at or near 100% duty cycle with rising junction temp confirms h4 directly; (b) a TEC operating within normal load parameters but receiving a sagging supply voltage points to hyp.upstream_power; (c) a TEC that is simply off or faulted (zero drive) also confirms h4. This result moves h4 and hyp.upstream_power in opposite directions — the single most discriminating split available given current evidence — while h1, h2, and h5 remain less necessary to invoke until the thermal/power root cause is resolved.

Both h4 (isolated TEC/thermal fault) and hyp.upstream_power (shared upstream cause) remain active. ev.laser_power_01 and ev.tec_load_01 confirm thermal runaway on this channel but cannot distinguish whether the TEC is failing in isolation or whether a shared power/thermal supply anomaly is starving the TEC of drive headroom across multiple channels. A common_mode_check is the single most discriminating action: if degradation is present on other channels simultaneously, it strongly supports hyp.upstream_power (shared upstream cause) and reframes h4 as a downstream symptom rather than root cause; if the effect is strictly isolated to this channel, hyp.upstream_power falls and h4 (component-level TEC failure) is confirmed as the root cause requiring a swap/repair on this unit alone. Running laser_power_check or tec_load_check again would only re-confirm already well-evidenced findings without moving the two hypotheses apart.
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential

title: TEC degradation
caption: worked example: cross-subsystem + decoy + lying channel + demoted trigger
